fuzzy_prd: We need a reusable type-ahead text field component. As a user types, a list of matching suggestions should appear below the field and update in real time. The user should be able to navigate the list with arrow keys, pick a suggestion with Enter or by clicking, and dismiss the list by pressing Escape or clicking away. When an item is highlighted by keyboard the field text should mirror that item; when the highlight is cleared (by pressing past the last item) the original typed text should be restored. Mouse hover should also highlight items, and mousing away should clear the highlight. The component needs to support both a flat list and a grouped multi-section layout. The matching logic uses a case-insensitive prefix rule — look at how the existing search bar in the app filters candidates and apply the same trimming convention. The component must emit notifications about focus, blur, value changes (including which interaction method caused the change), selection events, and requests to fetch or clear suggestions. Keyboard focus should stay on the field after clicking a suggestion by default, and an option should exist to change that. Accessibility roles and attributes must be maintained throughout.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with config/actions/observe) -> stdout autosuggest state/render/callback transcript

trigger_keywords: how many items before the wrap-around in arrow navigation
how many Down presses to clear highlight
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The Down cycle has count+1 steps: Down from item 0 moves to item 1, ..., Down from item (count-1) moves to the 'none' state, and one more Down returns to item 0. Up is the mirror: Up from item 0 goes to 'none', then to item (count-1).

trigger_keywords: what is the default shouldRender predicate
when do suggestions become eligible to show
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Default shouldRender ('default'): suggestions are eligible when the trimmed value is non-empty. 'nonEmptyNoLeadingSpace': trimmed value non-empty AND first character is not a space. 'alwaysTrue': always eligible.

trigger_keywords: what happens when focusInputOnSuggestionClick is false
does blur fire when focus not retained
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When focusInputOnSuggestionClick is false, clicking a suggestion does not return keyboard focus to the input field, and the component emits a blur notification with focusedSuggestion set to the clicked suggestion's name.

trigger_keywords: what method values are emitted in change notifications
which interaction triggers which method name
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Change notification method values: 'type' (user typed), 'down' (arrow down moved highlight), 'up' (arrow up moved highlight), 'click' (suggestion clicked), 'enter' (Enter pressed to select), 'escape' (Escape pressed to revert).

trigger_keywords: async mode delay and stale result handling
how long does async mode wait before delivering results
does async mode discard outdated results
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: In async mode (async: true), the host delivers match results after a 100ms delay. If the current input value has changed since the fetch was triggered, the results are discarded (stale results never update the suggestion list).

trigger_keywords: what role does the suggestions list have
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The input element has role='combobox'. The suggestions container has role='listbox'. Each individual suggestion has role='option'.

Full React Autosuggest behavior: typed input changes, suggestion filtering, two-stage Escape restore/clear, Enter and click selection, fetch and clear callback triggering, autoFocusFirstSuggestion, always-rendered suggestions, multi-section grouping, query-aware rendering, attributes/ARIA roles, and deterministic rendered output/state observations.

The adapter executes ordered actions against the configured autosuggest scenario and emits requested observations. Public_test_cases define exact action names, callback counts, highlighted/selected values, rendered text, grouped sections, and attribute pass-through.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, and deterministic formatting. When prose and expected_output differ, expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the same trimming convention as the existing search bar
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The case-insensitive prefix matching rule: trim the typed value, then include any candidate whose 'name' field starts with the trimmed value (case-insensitive). The matching is defined in the dataset host callback wired in the driver.

oracle_data.fallback_response: Consult the src/ directory for the component's state machine and the rcb_tests/dispatcher/ for the full scenario-driven adapter showing all config options and observation keys.

feature10_focus_first_suggestion.json.description: When configured to auto-focus the first suggestion, typing a value that yields matches immediately highlights the first item (and keeps highlighting the first item when the same matches are shown again, or after blur+refocus). From this auto-focused state, Down advances to the second item and one more Down past the last clears the highlight. Pressing Enter selects the auto-focused item and hides suggestions; pressing Enter when there are no suggestions simply leaves the typed value unchanged.

feature11_always_render_suggestions.json.description: When configured to always render suggestions, the matching list is visible from the start and stays visible through focus and blur. Typing narrows the list to the matches. Arrow keys still highlight items. Pressing Enter on a highlighted item re-fetches around the selected value (so the list updates to just that item) but does not hide the list; Enter with nothing highlighted leaves the full match list shown. Escape without prior navigation clears the field; Escape after Up/Down reverts the value and removes the highlight. Clicking an item updates the list to that item but keeps suggestions shown.

cases.expected_output:
suggestions=14
  [0] C
  [1] C#
  [2] C++
  [3] Clojure
  [4] Elm
  [5] Go
  [6] Haskell
  [7] Java
  [8] Javascript
  [9] Perl
  [10] PHP
  [11] Python
  [12] Ruby
  [13] Scala

feature12_multi_section.json.description: In multi-section mode suggestions are organised into titled groups. The visible suggestions are the flattened concatenation of all non-empty groups in order, and each visible group renders a section title. A selection event reports the index of the section the chosen item belongs to (0-based). The flat highlight index produced by arrow keys maps onto the correct section. A host 'clear' control can reset the field and re-show the full set of grouped suggestions.

cases.expected_output:
suggestions=14
  [0] C
  [1] C#
  [2] C++
  [3] Clojure
  [4] Elm
  [5] Go
  [6] Haskell
  [7] Java
  [8] Javascript
  [9] Perl
  [10] PHP
  [11] Python
  [12] Ruby
  [13] Scala

titles=1
  [0] C
suggestions=4
  [0] C
  [1] C#
  [2] C++
  [3] Clojure

feature13_async_suggestions.json.description: When the host resolves suggestions asynchronously (here after a 100ms timer) the list only appears once time advances. If the value changes before the timer fires, stale results are discarded so only suggestions matching the current value appear. After a selection (click or Enter) the suggestions are hidden and remain hidden even after further time passes.

feature14_suggestion_highlight_query.jso.description: Each suggestion is rendered with the portion that matches the current query wrapped in emphasis markup and the remainder in plain markup. The query handed to the renderer is the trimmed value the user originally typed: it is the value-before-navigation while Up/Down navigation is active (so navigating does not change which characters are highlighted), and it is unaffected by hovering a suggestion and then leaving it.

feature15_aria_attributes.json.description: The text field exposes combobox accessibility semantics: a role of 'combobox', list autocomplete, and an expanded flag that is 'false' when no suggestions are shown and 'true' when they are. When suggestions are open the list exposes a 'listbox' role and each option a role of 'option'. The active-descendant pointer is absent until a suggestion is highlighted (via keyboard or mouse) and becomes present while one is highlighted, returning to absent when the highlight is cleared.

cases.expected_output:
inputAttr role=combobox
inputAttr aria-autocomplete=list
expanded=false
activedescendant=absent

feature16_input_props_passthrough.json.description: Attributes supplied by the host for the text field (its id, placeholder and input type) are passed through to the rendered input element, and the field's displayed value tracks the host-provided value.

cases.expected_output:
inputAttr id=host-input
inputAttr placeholder=Type a value
inputAttr type=search
value=

feature1_show_hide_suggestions.json.description: Drive the component through focusing the text field and typing, then observe which suggestions are visible. Suggestions become visible only while the field is focused, the typed value passes the host's render predicate, and at least one candidate matches the value as a case-insensitive prefix. Pressing Escape or blurring the field hides them; focusing again re-shows the matches. A value that fails the render predicate (e.g. one that begins with a space) or that matches nothing yields no visible suggestions.

feature2_keyboard_navigation.json.description: With suggestions visible, the Down and Up arrow keys move a highlight through the list. Down starts at the first item and advances; after passing the last item the highlight wraps to 'none' and then back to the first. Up starts at the last item and moves backward, wrapping symmetrically. While an item is highlighted the field's displayed value mirrors that item's value. If suggestions are currently hidden, the first arrow press only reveals them again with no item highlighted.

feature3_mouse_focus.json.description: Moving the mouse onto a suggestion highlights it; moving the mouse off clears the highlight. After highlighting an item by mouse, pressing Up from the first item (or Down from the last item) restores the originally typed value rather than an item value.

feature4_escape_behavior.json.description: Escape has two stages. When suggestions are visible, the first Escape hides them without changing the typed value. A subsequent Escape (now that suggestions are hidden) clears the field to empty. If suggestions were never shown for the value, a single Escape clears the field. If the user had navigated with Up/Down first, Escape instead reverts the field to the value typed before navigation.

feature5_select_with_enter.json.description: Pressing Enter while a suggestion is highlighted selects it: a selection event is emitted carrying the selected value, the section index (null for a flat list) and the method 'enter', and the suggestions are hidden afterwards. The displayed value reflects the highlighted item. Pressing Enter with no highlighted suggestion emits no selection event but still hides the suggestions. The events log here is captured from the moment just before Enter is pressed.

cases.expected_output:
value=Java
suggestions=0
events=3
  change method=down newValue=Java
  suggestionSelected value=Java section=null method=enter
  clear

feature6_select_with_click.json.description: Clicking a suggestion selects it: the displayed value becomes the clicked item's value, a change event with method 'click' is emitted followed by a selection event with method 'click', the suggestions are then cleared, and (by default) keyboard focus is returned to the text field. The events log here is captured from the moment just before the click.

cases.expected_output:
value=PHP
suggestions=0
inputFocused=true
events=3
  change method=click newValue=PHP
  suggestionSelected value=PHP section=null method=click
  clear

feature7_change_event_methods.json.description: Every programmatic change to the field's value is reported through a change event tagged with the method that caused it: 'type' when the user edits the text, 'down'/'up' when navigation moves the value to a different item, 'click' when a suggestion is clicked, and 'escape' when Escape resets the value. No change event is emitted when the resulting value would be identical to the current value (for example navigating onto an item whose value already equals the field). The events log here is captured from the moment just before the final interaction.

cases.expected_output:
events=2
  change method=type newValue=c+
  fetch value=c+

feature8_fetch_and_clear_requests.json.description: The component asks the host to fetch suggestions (passing the current value) and to clear suggestions, at well-defined moments. A fetch is requested when the user types a value that passes the render predicate. Clearing is requested when the field is blurred, when Escape closes the suggestions, and when the typed value fails the render predicate. The events log here is captured from a reset point so only the events caused by the final interaction are shown; focus and blur notifications are included.

cases.expected_output:
events=2
  change method=type newValue=j
  fetch value=j

feature9_blur_on_click_when_focus_not_re.description: When the component is configured NOT to return focus to the field after a suggestion click, clicking a suggestion leaves the field unfocused and emits a blur event whose payload reports the suggestion that was focused at blur time (the clicked item). A clear-suggestions request is also emitted. The events log here is captured from just before the click.

cases.expected_output:
inputFocused=false
events=4
  change method=click newValue=PHP
  suggestionSelected value=PHP section=null method=click
  clear
  blur focusedSuggestion=PHP
